subject: Geology
title: Mass Wasting
description: Learn Mass Wasting in one minute.

hook.text: A hillside collapses after heavy rain. Tons of mud, rock, and debris race downhill. Gravity never stops pulling.

buildup.text: Mass wasting is the downslope movement of rock and soil under gravity. It includes landslides, mudflows, and creep.

discovery.text: Creep is the slowest form: soil inches downhill over years. Tilted fences and curved tree trunks reveal its progress.

twist.text: Water is the biggest trigger. Saturated soil is heavy and slippery. Most catastrophic landslides follow heavy rainfall.

climax.text: Mass wasting moves material from highlands to lowlands. It links mountain erosion to valley deposition via gravity.

quiz.question: What is the main force driving mass wasting?
